title: Actor Schema (NPC + Enemy unified envelope)
description: Canonical JSON-Schema for every actor — NPCs (ambient / interactive / vendor / dispatcher / hero) and enemies (combatant, reanimatable). Authoring tools (tools/npc-designer.html, tools/enemy-hydrator.html, etc.) validate every edit against this document. Runtime shape is documented in engine/npc-system.js lines 75-224 and data/enemies.json. See docs/NPC_TOOLING_ROADMAP.md (DOC-110) §5.2.

floorId.description: Dotted depth path (CLAUDE.md §Floor hierarchy). 'N' = exterior, 'N.N' = interior, 'N.N.N' = nested dungeon.

suit.description: Playing-card suit used by the combat triangle (CLAUDE.md §Project identity).

gateCheck.description: Forward-compat metadata for NPCs that gate a locked door on journal-book presence. Canonical consumer: floor3_inspector (Game._interact post-dialogue hook).

verbEntry.description: Single verb of a VerbSet (VERB_FIELD_NPC_ROADMAP §5.1). Decay drives the NPC toward satisfiers.

decayRate.description: Need units gained per ms. Typical 0.0004–0.0030.

satisfiers.description: Node-type ids this verb orbits (bonfire, well, bench, shop_entrance, bulletin_board, faction_post, work_station, rest_spot, soup_kitchen, food_stall, cot).

factionLock.description: If set, this verb only satisfies at nodes whose factionId matches.

floorScope.description: POST_JAM Wave 3 #1 — where the actor will search for satisfiers. Default 'local' when omitted.

attenuationCurve.description: Optional per-verb distance-weight override. Omit to use VerbField DISTANCE_WEIGHT default.

verbSet.description: Map of verb-name → verbEntry. Canonical verbs: rest, social, duty, errands, eat. Custom verbs permitted for Act 2 archetypes.

effect.description: Named effect callback (flag-set, currency, shop open, etc.).

reanimTier.description: VERB_FIELD_NPC_ROADMAP §18 reanimation tiers. T1 = wander only, T2 = dialogue tree, T3 = dispatch → cage pipeline.

dialogueTreeId.description: Required for T2/T3. Key into dialogueTree registry.

dispatchTarget.description: Required for T3. Describes the cage + processed-shop pipeline target.

disposition.description: D3_AI_LIVING_INFRA_PROCGEN_AUDIT §3 — unified disposition across NPCs + reanimated enemies. friendly = non-combat interactable; hostile_unaware = patrols without LOS; hostile_alerted = pursuit / combat.

movementMode.description: How the actor moves at tick time. verb_field pulls toward satisfier nodes; patrol runs two-point bounce; pursuit chases the player via Pathfind.find() (not yet implemented — reserved for Hero NPCs); stationary parks at (x,y); dispatcher_choreography uses the DispatcherChoreography module.

waypointMode.description: Reservation field for Hero NPC pathfinding (DOC-9 §8.3). Hero NPCs require multi-floor Pathfind.find() which is not yet implemented. Until it ships, Hero authoring is deferred and this field stays 'none' or 'patrol_bounce'.

hazardPolicy.description: D3 §2 hazard-cost integration. avoid = pathfinding penalty > 0; ignore = no penalty; use = enemy weaponises hazards against the player.

webPolicy.description: D3 §2 cobweb/viscosity policy. slow = speed reduced but path unchanged; attenuate = path cost increased; ignore = move freely.

floorScope.description: POST_JAM Wave 3 #1 cross-floor verb attenuation. local = satisfiers must be on the same floor; cross_floor = actor will path across floors (T3 dispatch behavior); none = verb does not drive movement. Default 'local' preserves Act 1 behavior.

attenuationCurve.description: Optional per-verb attenuation curve. Array of [distance, weight] pairs in ascending distance order. Weight 0–1; 1 = full pull, 0 = no pull. Overrides DISTANCE_WEIGHT=0.15 default when present. Reserved — implementation blocked on VerbField tick refactor.

entityBrain.description: D3_AI_LIVING_INFRA_PROCGEN_AUDIT §3 — unified EntityBrain contract. Reserved on every actor (NPC + enemy) so the D3 reliability pass can migrate runtime behaviors to a single shape. Currently informational; VerbField + EnemyAI read legacy fields (verbSet, patrolPoints, awarenessRange) until the Phase 1 reanim brain upgrade flips the read path.

dispatcherPhase.description: ACT2_NARRATIVE_OUTLINE §5 dispatcher state machine. normal = Act 1 baseline; faltering = Act 2 deterioration (missed briefings, faction pressure); missing = dispatcher absent from post; replaced = double-agent replacement has taken over. Transitions are quest-step driven (not day-tick) per audit risk G decision.

housingStatus.description: ACT2_NARRATIVE_OUTLINE §5.4 housing reassignment arc. Tracks Gleaner's current housing anchor as the dispatcher arc unfolds. home16_reclaimed = Act 2 ending state where Gleaner has clawed back their original bunk.

housingAffinity.description: ACT2 §5.4 affinity tier controlling interior amenity caps. faction = aligned housing (max amenities + faction emblem); civilian = neutral assignment (baseline amenities); fragmented = transient/emergency (minimum amenities per COZY_INTERIORS Safety Contract — bed + save + stash + 1 utility).

questRole.description: DOC-107 quest anchor + DOC-110 §9 population planner filter. Distinguishes narrative-beat NPCs from world-dressing ambient NPCs. Every NPC referenced by a data/quests.json anchor must carry a non-ambient questRole.

npcActor.description: NPC actor envelope. Maps 1:1 to NpcSystem._normalise() output (engine/npc-system.js:185-227).

stack.description: NpcComposer sprite stack. null → seed-driven from id.

patrolPoints.description: Two-point bounce patrol. null → stationary or verb-field driven.

barkPool.description: BarkLibrary pool key for proximity barks.

verbArchetype.description: Key into archetype-registry.js. Resolves to a verbSet at spawn.

brain.description: D3 unified EntityBrain contract. Reserved — runtime currently reads legacy fields (patrolPoints, verbSet) until Phase 1 reanim brain migration.

dispatcherPhase.description: ACT2 dispatcher state machine. Only meaningful on type='dispatcher'. Omit/null for Act 1 NPCs.

housingStatus.description: ACT2 §5.4 housing reassignment state. Only meaningful on the Gleaner player-bunk NPC and dispatcher-assigned residents.

home16Locked.description: ACT2 §5.4 — when true, Gleaner's original bunk is reclaimed by an NPC and the DOOR lock is active. Flips false on home16_reclaimed ending.

housingAffinity.description: ACT2 §5.4 housing-tier affinity. Drives interior amenity cap during COZY_INTERIORS pass.

voiceType.description: POST_JAM Wave 3 #5 voice-line tag. Matches BarkLibrary voice bank.

questRole.description: DOC-107 anchor marker + DOC-110 §9 population filter.

meetPoolId.description: DOC-110 P2 Bark Workbench — pool key for first-meet introductory lines, keyed by (faction, questRole).

portraitAssetId.description: DOC-110 P5 Sprite Studio — key into portrait atlas. Used by DialogBox when rendering interactive NPCs.

waypointMode.description: Hero NPC pathfinding reservation (DOC-9 §8.3). Stays 'none' or 'patrol_bounce' until multi-floor Pathfind.find() ships.

enemyActor.description: Enemy actor envelope. Maps 1:1 to data/enemies.json rows. Ships with a lootProfile + deck + tier band referenced by enemy-intent.js / enemy-deck.js.

deckId.description: Key into data/enemy-decks.json. null → hydrator must assign.

reanimArchetype.description: Key into archetype-registry.js REANIM_ARCHETYPES. Read by engine/reanimated-behavior.js.classify().

brain.description: D3 unified EntityBrain contract. Reserved — EnemyAI currently reads legacy awareness / reanimArchetype until D3 reliability pass flips the read path.

_meta.revisionNote: v1.1.0 — DOC-110 Phase 0 Chapter 4 schema expansion. Added 12 new definitions (disposition, movementMode, waypointMode, hazardPolicy, webPolicy, floorScope, attenuationCurve, entityBrain, dispatcherPhase, housingStatus, housingAffinity, questRole) and wired them into verbEntry (floorScope + attenuationCurve), npcActor (brain, dispatcherPhase, housingStatus, home16Locked, housingAffinity, voiceType, questRole, meetPoolId, portraitAssetId, waypointMode), and enemyActor (brain). Commits to cross-roadmap contracts surfaced by NPC_TOOLING_DEPENDENCY_AUDIT so DOC-110 Phase 1+ can proceed without re-shaping the schema.
